title: Jurisdiction Rule Profile Switching
description: A risk-and-compliance control workflow that sequences screening, escalation, and approval gates before transaction progression. Focus case: jurisdiction rule profile switching.
assumptions: Case-level operations are atomic per case_instance: state transitions are applied consistently and auditably.
Approval policy objects are authoritative and encode required reviewer roles; reviewer identities are distinct from policy objects.
Each profile_candidate and jurisdiction_code is uniquely identifiable; conflicts are resolved by policy or escalation.
Required reviewer/SME resources are available within operational SLAs to satisfy approval gates.
Audit and logging mechanisms capture each binding, unbinding, escalation, and policy engagement for post-hoc review.
risks: Race conditions when multiple profile_candidate bindings or reviewer engagements occur concurrently on the same case_instance.
Policy conflict risk where multiple approval_policy objects impose contradictory requirements for the same case.
Misclassification of case attributes leading to incorrect candidate selection and subsequent improper rule application.
Segregation-of-duties violations if the same actor can both propose and approve a profile switch without enforced controls.
Naming and conceptual ambiguity between policy objects and actor roles (policy vs. personnel) can lead to incorrect mappings in governance documentation and automation.
